title: Deck Equipment and Fittings Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: Saudi steel fabricators upgraded to Lloyd's Register naval standards through PSAP Track 3
executive_summary:
Deck equipment and fittings — bollards, fairleads, cleats, capstans, anchor windlasses, hatches, and structural deck fittings — are the unglamorous but essential hardware of naval vessels. They are manufactured using standard steel fabrication processes (cutting, forming, welding, machining) to Lloyd's Register or equivalent naval classification standards. Saudi Arabia's existing steel fabrication base — anchored by the Jubail heavy industrial complex and SABIC's Hadeed steel — is directly adjacent to this capability.

Node NV-3 is the most immediately accessible manufacturing entry point in the naval sector, for exactly the same reason as MA-3 (GSE) in aerospace: standard manufacturing processes, non-ITAR content, achievable certification timeline, and immediate demand from the Avante 2200 programme. Saudi-manufactured deck fittings would be the first Saudi-made hardware installed aboard a Royal Saudi Navy warship.

The global naval deck equipment market is approximately $1.8 billion annually. Saudi Arabia's naval fittings opportunity is modest in dollar terms — $15–30 million over the Avante 2200 programme — but the strategic value is the precedent: a Saudi Lloyd's Register-certified naval hardware manufacturer that creates the supply chain foundation for more complex naval equipment in Phase II.

companies.name: Rolls-Royce Marine (Kongsberg)
companies.rationale: Kongsberg/Rolls-Royce Marine produces deck machinery and naval equipment. A technical licensing arrangement for standard deck fittings gives Saudi manufacturers validated designs.
companies.contact: Kongsberg Maritime VP International Supply Chain

companies.name: Macgregor (Cargotec)
companies.rationale: Macgregor produces marine and naval deck equipment globally. Their licensing model supports local manufacturing in many countries.

companies.name: Fluidmecanica
companies.rationale: As a Navantia supply chain partner, Fluidmecanica could provide technical specifications for Avante 2200 compatible fittings and transition work to a Saudi manufacturer.

companies.name: Italwinch
companies.rationale: Italian naval deck machinery specialist; non-ITAR; active international sales.

companies.name: Karmøy Winch
companies.rationale: Norwegian naval deck equipment manufacturer; established regional relationships; licensing model available.
companies.contact: Karmøy Winch VP Sales and Partnerships

partners.name: Al-Zamil Steel / Zamil Industrial
partners.current: Structural steel fabrication; CNC cutting and forming; welding to industrial standards
partners.upgrade: Lloyd's Register certification for naval deck fittings; marine-grade stainless steel and carbon steel fabrication; dimensional and NDT compliance for naval standards
partners.timeline: 15 months to first Lloyd's Register-certified delivery

partners.name: SABIC / Hadeed
partners.upgrade: Supply marine-grade steel and stainless steel to Saudi deck equipment manufacturers; establish Lloyd's Register material certification

partners.name: Saudi Heavy Industries (SHI — Jubail)
partners.current: Heavy fabrication for offshore and petrochemical; proven marine fabrication experience
partners.upgrade: Naval-grade welding qualification; Lloyd's Register supplier approval; establish dedicated naval fittings production cell

partners.name: TVTC Jubail Campus
partners.current: Welding and fabrication training programmes; some offshore industry training
partners.upgrade: Naval-grade welding certification (Lloyd's Register approved); marine coatings training; quality control for naval fabrication
